reasoning: The crowd forecast is at 30.09%. New export controls on AI (advanced chips and frontier model weights) were just implemented in January 2025. For another package of broad, globally applied export controls expanding these restrictions or introducing cloud controls to be drafted, cleared, published, and reach its effective date by December 31, 2025, seems relatively constrained. Furthermore, 2025 is a transition year of a new presidential administration. It generally takes several months to appoint and confirm new leadership at the Commerce Department (BIS), who would then need time to review policy frameworks or the Jan 2025 rules before driving completely new restrictions. While an AI advancement catalyst or hawkish actions against China could spur an emergency interim rule, a broad foundational export control update within less than 11 months is a heavy administrative lift. I estimate the probability slightly below the crowd average due to the tight requirement for an effective date in 2025 and bureaucratic inertia.
